scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Meets all pass criteria: 7 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings, 5+ escalations serving as clarifying questions, 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined in escalations and low_confidence_areas, no Given/When/Then acceptance criteria written (acceptance_criteria array is empty), overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values were invented as requirements.

observations.text: 7 well-structured business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Demand is explicitly flagged as undefined in the first escalation with detailed analysis of what it could mean
acceptance_criteria array is empty — Bird correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
confidence.level of 30 appropriately reflects the massive ambiguity in the spec
5 escalations with structured options and recommendations serve as thorough clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrails, competitiveness, courier pay, and regulatory review
Edge cases section identifies 7 realistic scenarios without inventing specific parameter values
Low confidence areas list 10 undefined aspects, demonstrating thorough ambiguity detection
BR-6 (deterministic pricing) and BR-7 (auditability) are thoughtful additions beyond what the CEO stated, correctly marked as invariants with strong justification
BR-4 marks price ceiling as invariant:true despite the CEO saying 'probably' — Bird justifies this well but could have noted it as an assumption more prominently given the hedge
Stakeholder impact analysis identifies courier/driver compensation gap — a critical missing element not in the original spec
